texto: I try to never miss the Zumba class since I believe comedy is a great way to start your week....It was great fun, although I had to hold myself back from turning it into a giant mosh pit slam dance. Last time I did that they kicked me out for a month. Today I enjoyed watching an 80 year old lady - with a cane no less - shimmy, her boobs flying like weapons around her waist.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Debra Dunbar: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I try to never miss the Zumba class since I believe comedy is a great way to start your week....I…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, comedy, demons pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de demons e imps.

frase_original: I try to never miss the Zumba class since I believe comedy is a great way to start your week....It was great fun, although I had to hold myself back from turning it into a giant mosh pit slam dance. Last time I did that they kicked me out for a month. Today I enjoyed watching an 80 year old lady - with a cane no less - shimmy, her boobs flying like weapons around her waist.

texto: Do I look like I'm compelled to do anything? Do I seriously look like anyone could compel me to even bring them a coaster?"She looked me over again. "You look like you're compelled to cause trouble, but I doubt that's the boss' directive.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Debra Dunbar: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a humor. O trecho “Do I look like I'm compelled to do anything? Do I seriously look like anyone could compel me to e…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que humor, compelled, humorous pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de humorous e troublemaker.
